memoryArtifacts.headline: Sviatopolk II, Grand Prince of Kiev.
St. Michael's Golden-Domed Monastery, Kiev, Ukraine. Photo added to Find a Grave by Angie Swann on 4 December 2015.
Mosaic of St. Demetrius was installed by Sviatopolk in Kievan St. Michael's Golden-Domed Monastery to glorify the patron saint of his father
The reconstructed monastery with cathedral and bell tower seen in front of St. Michael's Square.
А statue of the Archangel Michael.

zh.lifeSummary: 當Svyatoslav Sviatopolk II Izyaslavich, Grand Prince of Kiev於1050年11月8日出生在烏克蘭Київ時，他的父親Iziaslav I Yaroslavich Grand Prince of Kyiv23歲，母親Gertrude of Poland26歲。他於大約1070年與Lady Svyatopolk II Duchess of Kiev x在烏克蘭結婚。他去世於1113年4月16日在Войско ЗапорожскоеКиївський полкКиївська сотняВишгород享年62歲，埋葬在烏克蘭Київ。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Svyatoslav Sviatopolk II Izyaslavich, Grand Prince of Kiev于1050年11月8日出生在乌克兰Київ时，他的父亲Iziaslav I Yaroslavich Grand Prince of Kyiv23岁，母亲Gertrude of Poland26岁。他于大约1070年与Lady Svyatopolk II Duchess of Kiev x在乌克兰结婚。他去世于1113年4月16日在Войско ЗапорожскоеКиївський полкКиївська сотняВишгород享年62岁，埋葬在乌克兰Київ。

nameMeanings.name: Grand
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: French: nickname from Old French grand, grant ‘tall, large’, from Latin grandis. See also Grant 1 and 4, compare Degrand and Legrand .
German: topographic name for someone who lived in a gravelly area, from Middle Low German grand ‘coarse gravel’.
German: nickname from a southern word of uncertain origin meaning ‘frustration, anger, ire’.

variants.name: Grant
meanings: Irish, English, and especially Scottish (of Norman origin): nickname from Anglo-Norman French graund, graunt ‘tall, large’ (Old French grand, grant, from Latin grandis), given either to a person of remarkable size, or else in a relative way to distinguish two bearers of the same personal name, often representatives of different generations within the same family.
English: from the rare Middle English (and Old English) personal name Grante or Grente.
Irish: in Ireland this is usually the Norman Scottish name (see 1 above), but it was also adopted for Irish Mag Raighne, see Graney .

variants.name: Degrand
meanings: French: patronymic, with fused preposition de ‘of’, from the nickname Grand ‘tall, large’.

American shortened form of Walloon Degrandgagnage, itself a variant, with fused preposition de ‘from’, of Grandgagnage, a habitational name from Grandgagnage in Wallonia (Belgium). The surname Degrandgagnage is not found in Belgium.

variants.name: Grandin
meanings: French: nickname from a diminutive of   Grand   ‘big, tall’.
Italian: from a derivative of   Grande  .

nameMeanings.name: Prince
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and French: nickname from Middle English, Old French prince (from Latin princeps).
Americanized form (translation into English) of German and Jewish (Ashkenazic) Prinz ‘prince’, or its Czech, Slovak, Slovenian or some other Slavic cognate Princ, from princ ‘prince’.
Americanized form of Slovenian Primc: status name for one who lives in the house of his father-in-law, from a derivative of primiti ‘to accept, to take over’.

variants.name: Prinz
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Matthias, Ulrich, Arno, Friedrich, Hans, Klaus, Mechthild.

German: nickname from Middle High German prinze ‘prince’, presumably denoting someone who behaved in a regal manner or who had won the title in some contest of skill. This surname is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine) and Czechia.
Jewish (Ashkenazic): adoption of German Prinz ‘prince’.

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Cheng, Jing, Kam Fung, Wai Shing, Wing, Yali, Yuk.
Chinese:
variant Romanization of the surnames 歐 and 區, see   Ou   1 and 2.

variants.name: Ou
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ying, Ching, Heng, Hong, Jia, Kuang Yu, Li-Ching, Cho, Chong, Chung, Sa.  Southeast Asian Lichun, Ming, Quan, Roeun, Sokhom, Chau, Hai, Leang, Ly, Nhanh, Phan, Pho, Tha.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 歐, meaning ‘to sing someone's praise’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from the placename Dong Ou (東甌), a fief (in present-day Wenzhou in Zhejiang province) which was granted to Yao in 192  BC . It is said that Yao was a descendant of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from the placename Ou Yu (歐餘), the name of a fief (located in present-day Zhejiang province) that was granted to Ti, the second son of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 區, originally denoting an ancient vessel for measurement in ancient Chinese: from the personal name of Ou Zhizi (歐治子), an expert sword-maker who lived during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ). Some of his descendants inherited Ou (歐) as their surname, simplifying the Chinese character 歐 to 區.

nameMeanings.name: Kiev
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Austrian German: occupational name for a cowherd, Chüyger in the Tyrolean dialect, from Kühe ‘cows’ (plural of Kuh) + -er suffix of agent nouns.
English (Lancashire): variant of   Kear  .
Scottish (Ayrshire and Lanarkshire): variant of   Keir  .

variants.name: Kear
meanings: English (Gloucestershire): occupational name for a locksmith, from Middle English keyer, kayer ‘key-maker, locksmith’ (Old English cǣgere, a derivative of cǣg ‘key’).

nameMeanings.name: Svyatoslav
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Slavic: from svyanto ‘bright, holy’ + slav ‘glory’.

nameMeanings.name: II
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Violet  ,   Vivien  , or   Vivian .

variants.name: Vivien
meanings: Earlier generally taken as a variant of the boy's name Vivian , but now also rarely used for boys. This spelling was quite common in Old French. Its use as a girl's name in the English-speaking world was influenced by Tennyson's Merlin and Vivien ( 1859 ). This name, from Arthurian legend, may represent an altered form of a Celtic name (perhaps akin to the Irish Gaelic name Béibhinn ‘white lady’, pronounced ‘bee-veen’). The actress Vivien Leigh ( 1913–67 ) was christened Vivian.

variants.name: Violet
meanings: From the name of the flower (Old French violette, Late Latin violetta, a diminutive of viola). This was one of the earliest flower names to become popular in Britain, being used as early as 1700 and becoming well established in the 19th century, although it is now somewhat out of favour.

variants.name: Vivian
meanings: Originally a boy's name, from an Old French form of the Latin name Vivianus (probably a derivative of vivus ‘alive’), but now more frequent as a girl's name. The name was borne by a 5th-century bishop of Saintes in western France, remembered for protecting his people during the invasion of the Visigoths.

en.lifeSummary: When Svyatoslav Sviatopolk II Izyaslavich, Grand Prince of Kiev was born on 8 November 1050, in Kyiv, Ukraine, his father, Iziaslav I Yaroslavich Grand Prince of Kyiv, was 23 and his mother, Gertrude of Poland, was 26. He married Lady Svyatopolk II Duchess of Kiev x about 1070, in Ukraine. He died on 16 April 1113, in Vyshgorod, Kievskaya sotnya, Kievskiy polk, Zaporizhian Host, at the age of 62, and was buried in Kyiv, Ukraine.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Svyatoslav Sviatopolk II Izyaslavich, Grand Prince of Kiev est né le 8 novembre 1050, à Kyiv, Ukraine, son père, Iziaslav I Yaroslavich Grand Prince of Kyiv, avait 23 ans et sa mère, Gertrude of Poland, avait 26 ans. Il a épousé Lady Svyatopolk II Duchess of Kiev x environ 1070, à Ukraine. Il est décédé le 16 avril 1113, à Vyshgorod, Kievskaya sotnya, Kievskiy polk, Kosakenhetmanat, à l’âge de 62 ans, et a été enterré à Kyiv, Ukraine.

de.lifeSummary: Als Svyatoslav Sviatopolk II Izyaslavich, Grand Prince of Kiev am 8. November 1050 in Kyiv, Ukraine geboren wurde, war sein Vater Iziaslav I Yaroslavich Grand Prince of Kyiv 23 und seine Mutter Gertrude of Poland 26. Er hat um 1070 in Ukraine Lady Svyatopolk II Duchess of Kiev x geheiratet. Er starb am 16. April 1113 im Alter von 62 Jahren in Vyshgorod, Kievskaya sotnya, Kievskiy polk, Kosakenhetmanat und wurde in Kyiv, Ukraine begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Svyatoslav Sviatopolk II Izyaslavich, Grand Prince of Kiev è nato il il 8 novembre 1050 nel Kyiv, Ucraina, il suo padre, Iziaslav I Yaroslavich Grand Prince of Kyiv, aveva 23 anni e il suo madre, Gertrude of Poland, aveva 26 anni. Lui sposato Lady Svyatopolk II Duchess of Kiev x circa 1070, nel Ucraina. Morì il 16 aprile 1113, nel Vyshgorod, Kievskaya sotnya, Kievskiy polk, Kosakenhetmanat, all'età di 62 anni, e fu sepolto a Kyiv, Ucraina.

ja.lifeSummary: Svyatoslav Sviatopolk II Izyaslavich, Grand Prince of Kievが1050年11月8日にウクライナКиївで生まれた時，父Iziaslav I Yaroslavich Grand Prince of Kyivは23歳，母Gertrude of Polandは26歳であった。約1070年にウクライナでLady Svyatopolk II Duchess of Kiev xと結婚した。彼は1113年4月16日にВойско ЗапорожскоеКиївський полкКиївська сотняВишгородで62歳で死去し，ウクライナКиївにて埋葬された。

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Svyatoslav Sviatopolk II Izyaslavich, Grand Prince of Kiev nasceu em 8 de novembro de 1050, em Kyiv, Ucrânia, seu pai, Iziaslav I Yaroslavich Grand Prince of Kyiv, tinha 23 anos e sua mãe, Gertrude of Poland, tinha 26 anos. Ele casou-se com Lady Svyatopolk II Duchess of Kiev x aproximadamente 1070, em Ucrânia. Ele faleceu em 16 de abril de 1113, em Vyshgorod, Kievskaya sotnya, Kievskiy polk, Kosakenhetmanat, com 62 anos, e foi sepultado em Kyiv, Ucrânia.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Svyatoslav Sviatopolk II Izyaslavich, Grand Prince of Kiev родился 8 ноября 1050 г. в местности Киев, Украина, его отцу по имени Iziaslav I Yaroslavich Grand Prince of Kyiv было 23 года, а матери, которую звали Gertrude of Poland, 26. Он женился на женщине по имени Lady Svyatopolk II Duchess of Kiev x около 1070 г. в местности Украина. Он умер 16 апреля 1113 г. в местности Вышгород, Киевская сотня, Киевский полк, Войско Запорожское в возрасте 62 лет и был похоронен в местности Киев, Украина.

Se casó con Lady Svyatopolk II Duchess of Kiev x aproximadamente 1070, en Ucrania. Murió el 16 de abril de 1113, en Vyshgorod, Kievskaya sotnya, Kievskiy polk, Kosakenhetmanat, a la edad de 62 años, y fue sepultado en Kyiv, Ucrania.
